 {"id":"gt-caih","title":"Witness handoff bead state persistence","description":"Implement state persistence for Witness across wisp cycles.

## Problem
Wisps burn between cycles, but Witness needs to remember:
- Which workers have been nudged
- How many times (nudge_count)
- When was last nudge
- Last observed activity

## Solution
Witness handoff bead with worker_states field:

```json
{
  "id": "gt-witness-state",
  "type": "handoff",
  "assignee": "<rig>/witness",
  "pinned": true,
  "worker_states": {
    "furiosa": {
      "issue": "gt-123",
      "nudge_count": 2,
      "last_nudge": "2024-12-22T10:00:00Z"
    }
  },
  "last_patrol": "2024-12-22T10:05:00Z"
}
```

## Implementation
1. On patrol start: bd show <witness-handoff-id> to load state
2. During patrol: update in-memory state
3. On save-state step: bd update to persist
4. State survives wisp burn/squash

## Depends on
- gt-83k0 (mol-witness-patrol definition)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:42:57.427131-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:16:16.78731-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:16:16.78731-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-caih","depends_on_id":"gt-83k0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:59.609821-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-caz","title":"Timed Beads: Scheduled recurring work","description":"## Summary

Timed beads wake up periodically and get injected into the ready queue by the daemon.

## Schema Extension

```yaml
id: gt-weekly-sync
type: task  # or sentinel
schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # cron: Monday 9am
# OR
interval: 24h          # every 24 hours
tier: haiku            # cheap model for routine checks
next_run: 2025-12-20T09:00:00Z
```

## Daemon Integration

Daemon heartbeat loop:
1. Check timed beads where `next_run <= now`
2. For each due bead:
   - Inject into ready queue (set status to open if needed)
   - Update `next_run` based on schedule/interval
3. Witnesses pick up work via `bd ready`

## Use Cases

- Weekly team sync reminders
- Daily health checks
- Periodic cleanup tasks
- Scheduled reports

## Interaction with Pinned Beads

A pinned bead can be timed - it wakes up periodically but never closes.
This is how you model "background services" in Gas Town.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-ci84","title":"Deacon patrol wisps should use town beads, not gastown rig beads","description":"## Problem

The deacon is a town-level role but stores patrol wisps in gastown/mayor/rig/.beads/ (see internal/cmd/prime.go:691). This fails for users who don't have the gastown rig installed.

## Solution

1. Town beads (~/gt/.beads/) should support wisps
2. Deacon patrol molecules should go to town beads with wisp flag
3. May need ~/gt/.beads-wisp/ for ephemeral town-level storage

## Files to fix

- internal/cmd/prime.go (outputDeaconPatrolContext)
- Possibly add town-level wisp directory initialization
- Update beads-data-plane.md to document correct architecture

## Correct architecture

Deacon -> ~/gt/.beads/ or ~/gt/.beads-wisp/ (town-level, no rig dependency)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:28:03.636334-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:28:24.651756-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-cik","title":"Overseer Crew: User-managed persistent workspaces","description":"## Overview

Crew workers are the overseer's (human's) personal workspaces within a rig. Unlike polecats which are witness-managed and ephemeral, crew workers are:

- **Persistent**: Not auto-garbage-collected
- **User-managed**: Overseer controls lifecycle
- **Long-lived identities**: dave, emma, fred - recognizable names
- **Gas Town integrated**: Mail, handoff mechanics work
- **Tmux optional**: Can work in terminal directly

## Directory Structure

```
<rig>/
  polecats/       # Managed workers (witness controls)
  refinery/       # Merge queue processor
  witness/        # Pit boss
  crew/           # Overseer's personal workspaces
    dave/         # Full clone, persistent
    emma/         # Full clone, persistent
    fred/         # Full clone, persistent
```

## Key Differences from Polecats

- Location: crew/ instead of polecats/
- Lifecycle: User-managed, not witness-managed
- Auto-cleanup: Never (polecats auto-cleanup on swarm land)
- Issue assignment: Optional (polecats require it)
- Tmux: Optional (polecats require it)
- Mail & Handoff: Yes for both
- Identity: Persistent (polecats are ephemeral)

## CLI Commands

- gt crew add <name> [--rig <rig>] - Create crew workspace
- gt crew list [--rig <rig>] - List crew workspaces
- gt crew at <rig>/<name> - Attach to workspace (start session)
- gt crew attach <name> - Attach (infer rig from cwd)
- gt crew refresh <name> - Handoff + restart (context cycling)
- gt crew remove <name> [--force] - Remove workspace
- gt crew status [<name>] - Show workspace status

## Design Notes

- Crew workers use full git clones (not worktrees)
- Optional beads integration via BEADS_DIR
- Mail-to-self handoff works for context cycling
- No witness monitoring or nudging
- No automatic issue assignment required

## Background

Users often maintain separate repo clones for serial agent work. This is tedious to set up manually. Crew workspaces bring these into Gas Town's infrastructure while keeping user control.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:47:37.529887-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-d46","title":"Mail CLI: archive, purge, search, mark","description":"GGT mail CLI needs more commands for mail management.

## Commands to Add

### gt mail check
Check for new mail without full inbox display.
```
gt mail check [--quiet] [--inject]
```
- --quiet: Only output if new mail (for scripts)
- --inject: Send notification to running session

### gt mail mark
Change read status.
```
gt mail mark <id> --read
gt mail mark <id> --unread
```

### gt mail delete
Remove message from inbox.
```
gt mail delete <id> [--force]
```
- Confirm unless --force

### gt mail archive
Move old/read messages to archive.
```
gt mail archive [--older-than DAYS] [--all-read] [--dry-run]
```
- Creates inbox.jsonl.archive or separate archive.jsonl

### gt mail purge
Permanently delete archived messages.
```
gt mail purge [--older-than DAYS] [--dry-run] [--force]
```

### gt mail search
Find messages by content.
```
gt mail search <query> [--from SENDER] [--subject] [--body]
```

### gt mail reply
Reply to a message.
```
gt mail reply <id> -m BODY
```
- Auto-sets reply_to and thread_id
- Auto-addresses to original sender

## Files to Modify
- internal/cmd/mail.go: Add commands
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Add Archive(), Search(), Delete()

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands implemented
- [ ] Archive stores in separate file
- [ ] Search supports regex patterns
- [ ] Delete confirms by default","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:57.158136-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:04:32.922813-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-dapb","title":"mol-polecat-arm","description":"Single polecat inspection and action cycle.

This molecule is bonded dynamically by mol-witness-patrol's survey-workers step.
Each polecat being monitored gets one arm.

## Variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| polecat_name | Yes | Name of the polecat to inspect |
| rig | Yes | Rig containing the polecat |

## Steps

### capture
Capture recent tmux output for this polecat.

```bash
tmux capture-pane -t gt-{{rig}}-{{polecat_name}} -p | tail -50
```

Record:
- Last activity timestamp (when was last tool call?)
- Visible errors or stack traces
- Completion indicators ("Done", "Finished", etc.)

### assess
Categorize polecat state.

States:
- **working**: Recent tool calls, active processing
- **idle**: At prompt, no recent activity
- **error**: Showing errors or stack traces
- **requesting_shutdown**: Sent LIFECYCLE/Shutdown mail
- **done**: Showing completion indicators

Calculate: minutes since last activity.
Needs: capture

### load-history
Read nudge history for this polecat from patrol state.

```
nudge_count = state.nudges[{{polecat_name}}].count
last_nudge_time = state.nudges[{{polecat_name}}].timestamp
```

Needs: assess

### decide
Apply the nudge matrix to determine action.

| State | Idle Time | Nudge Count | Action |
|-------|-----------|-------------|--------|
| working | any | any | none |
| idle | <10min | any | none |
| idle | 10-15min | 0 | nudge-1 (gentle) |
| idle | 15-20min | 1 | nudge-2 (direct) |
| idle | 20+min | 2 | nudge-3 (final) |
| idle | any | 3 | escalate |
| error | any | any | assess-severity |
| requesting_shutdown | any | any | pre-kill-verify |
| done | any | any | pre-kill-verify |

Nudge text:
1. "How's progress? Need any help?"
2. "Please wrap up soon. What's blocking you?"
3. "Final check. Will escalate in 5 min if no response."

Record decision and rationale.
Needs: load-history

### execute
Take the decided action.

**nudge-N**:
```bash
tmux send-keys -t gt-{{rig}}-{{polecat_name}} "{{nudge_text}}" Enter
```

**pre-kill-verify**:
```bash
cd polecats/{{polecat_name}}
git status                    # Must be clean
git log origin/main..HEAD     # Check for unpushed
bd show <assigned-issue>      # Verify closed/deferred
```
If clean: kill session, remove worktree, delete branch
If dirty: record failure, retry next cycle

**escalate**:
```bash
gt mail send mayor/ -s "Escalation: {{polecat_name}} stuck" -m "..."
```

**none**: No action needed.

Record: action taken, result, updated nudge count.
Needs: decide

## Output

The arm completes with:
- action_taken: none | nudge-1 | nudge-2 | nudge-3 | killed | escalated
- result: success | failed | pending
- updated_state: New nudge count and timestamp

This data feeds back to the patrol's aggregate step.

 {"id":"gt-e9k","title":"Workspace cleanup: preflight and postflight","description":"Workspace preflight and postflight commands for clean state management.

## Preflight
```
gt preflight [--rig <rig>] [--dry-run]
```

Run before starting batch work:
1. Clean stale mail in inboxes
2. Check for stuck workers (warn)
3. Check rig health (polecats, refinery)
4. Verify git state is clean
5. Run bd sync to ensure beads current

## Postflight
```
gt postflight [--rig <rig>] [--archive-mail] [--dry-run]
```

Run after batch work completes:
1. Archive old mail with --archive-mail
2. Clean up stale integration branches
3. Sync beads
4. Report on rig state

## Implementation
```go
func Preflight(rigName string, dryRun bool) (*PreflightReport, error)
func Postflight(rigName string, opts PostflightOptions) (*PostflightReport, error)
```

## Report Structures
```go
type PreflightReport struct {
    MailCleaned    int
    RigHealthy     bool
    StuckWorkers   []string
    Warnings       []string
}

type PostflightReport struct {
    MailArchived   int
    BranchesCleaned int
    Warnings       []string
}
```

## Note

These are workspace maintenance commands, not tied to "swarm" lifecycle. Run them anytime to keep the rig clean.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Preflight cleans stale state
- [ ] Postflight archives old mail
- [ ] Both have --dry-run mode
- [ ] Clear reports of actions taken","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:15.997677-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:01.55269-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-ebl","title":"CLI: names commands for polecat naming pool","description":"Polecat naming pool for auto-generated names.

## Commands

### gt names generate
```
gt names generate [--count N]
```
Generate N names from pool.

### gt names add
```
gt names add <name>...
```
Add custom names to pool.

### gt names list
```
gt names list
```
Show available and used names.

### gt names reset
```
gt names reset [--keep-used]
```
Reset pool to defaults.

## Config File
<rig>/town/naming.json:
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "auto_refill": true,
  "refill_threshold": 5,
  "pool": {
    "available": ["Toast", "Nux", "Capable", ...],
    "used": ["Alice", "Bob"]
  }
}
```

## Default Pool
Mad Max themed: Toast, Nux, Capable, Furiosa, Immortan, etc.

## Integration
gt polecat add calls naming pool if no name given:
```go
if name == "" {
    name, err = naming.Generate(rigPath)
}
```

## New Package
internal/naming/
├── pool.go     # Pool management
└── defaults.go # Default name lists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-generate names on polecat add
- [ ] Track used vs available
- [ ] Auto-refill when low
- [ ] Custom names addable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:33.592129-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:13.882465-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-id36.6","title":"Deacon event/timer callbacks: reactive triggers","description":"
Implement event-triggered and timer-based callbacks in the Deacon.

## Timer Callbacks

Agents can schedule future wakes by mailing the Deacon:
```
Subject: TIMER: gastown/witness wake at 2025-12-20T16:00:00Z
Body: Please nudge me - I'm waiting for external dependency
```

Deacon processing:
1. Parse timer mail
2. Check if time has passed
3. If yes: mail the agent "WAKE: Timer fired"
4. Close the timer mail

## Event Subscriptions

Plugins/agents can subscribe to events:
```yaml
# In kernel bead or plugin config
event_triggers:
  - event: "issue.created"
    action: mail
    target: "plugins/work-oracle"
    
  - event: "mr.submitted"
    action: run
    plugin: "review-oracle"
    
  - event: "agent.stuck"
    action: escalate
    target: human
```

## Event Sources

Events come through mail to deacon/:
```
Subject: EVENT: issue.created gt-abc123
Body: New issue created in gastown rig
```

## Event Types

Initial event types:
- `issue.created` - New bead created
- `issue.closed` - Bead closed
- `mr.submitted` - Merge request submitted
- `mr.merged` - Merge request merged
- `agent.stuck` - Agent appears stuck (from health scan)
- `agent.failed` - Agent remediation failed

## Event Processing

During events step:
1. Read event mail from inbox
2. Match against subscriptions
3. Execute actions:
   - `mail`: Send mail to target
   - `run`: Execute plugin inline
   - `spawn`: Spawn subagent for plugin
   - `escalate`: Mail human

## Relation to Mail

Mail IS the event bus. No separate event system.
Events are just specially-formatted mail to deacon/.

 {"id":"gt-iib","title":"Architecture: Decentralized rig structure with per-rig agents","description":"## Decision

Adopt decentralized architecture where each rig contains all its agents (mayor/, witness/, refinery/, polecats/) rather than centralizing mayor clones at town level.

## Town Level Structure

```
~/ai/                              # Town root
├── config/                        # Town config (VISIBLE, not hidden)
│   ├── town.json                  # {"type": "town"}
│   ├── rigs.json                  # Registry of managed rigs
│   └── federation.json            # Wasteland config (future)
│
├── mayor/                         # Mayor's HOME at town level
│   ├── CLAUDE.md
│   ├── mail/inbox.jsonl
│   └── state.json
│
└── <rigs>/                        # Managed projects
```

## Rig Level Structure (e.g., wyvern)

```
wyvern/                            # Rig = clone of project repo
├── .git/info/exclude              # Gas Town adds: polecats/ refinery/ witness/ mayor/
├── .beads/                        # Beads (if project uses it)
├── [project files]                # Clean project code on main
│
├── polecats/                      # Worker clones
│   └── <name>/                    # Each is a git clone
│
├── refinery/
│   ├── rig/                       # Refinery's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
├── witness/                       # NEW: Per-rig pit boss
│   ├── rig/                       # Witness's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
└── mayor/
    ├── rig/                       # Mayor's clone for this rig
    └── state.json
```

## Key Decisions

1. **Visible config dir**: `config/` not `.gastown/` (models don't find hidden dirs)
2. **Witness per-rig**: Each rig has its own Witness (pit boss) with its own clone
3. **Mayor decentralized**: Mayor's clones live IN each rig at `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
4. **Minimal invasiveness**: Only `.git/info/exclude` modified, no commits to project
5. **Clone subdir name**: Keep `rig/` for consistency (refinery/rig/, witness/rig/, mayor/rig/)

## Role Detection

- Town root or mayor/ → Mayor (town level)
- Rig root → Mayor (canonical main)
- <rig>/mayor/rig/ → Mayor (rig-specific)
- <rig>/refinery/rig/ → Refinery
- <rig>/witness/rig/ → Witness
- <rig>/polecats/<name>/ → Polecat

## Migration from PGT

- `mayor/rigs/<rig>/` → `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
- `<rig>/town/` → eliminated (rig root IS the clone)
- Add `witness/` to each rig","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:19.913928-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.461186-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.461186-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-iib","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.374551-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-ilav","title":"Polecat Lifecycle Events: Refinery-Witness Coordination","description":"## Problem

Polecat work completion is currently treated as a single event (MR submission), but the true completion is when the Refinery merges the work. This creates a lifecycle gap:

1. Polecat submits MR → considered "done" by Witness
2. But MR might fail to merge (conflicts, test failures)
3. No one notifies anyone when merge actually succeeds

## Solution: Two-Event Lifecycle

**Event 1: MR Submitted**
- `gt mq submit` notifies Witness
- Witness verifies submission was clean
- Polecat can go idle (but worktree retained)
- If submission had issues, Witness can help

**Event 2: MR Merged**
- Refinery sends `LIFECYCLE: work merged` to Witness
- Witness updates polecat state to `merged`
- Worktree now eligible for full cleanup/recycling

## Village Sibling Watch

For robustness, patrol roles check on their siblings:
- Refinery checks Witness via `gt peek`
- Witness checks Refinery via `gt peek`
- Creates defense in depth - if primary notification fails, someone catches it

## Recovery

If Refinery misses sending lifecycle notification:
- Witness polecat-scan can detect: polecat in mr_submitted but MR shows merged
- Deacon orphan-check can detect: merged MRs with stale polecats","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:06:56.746185-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:07:07.719057-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-jpt","title":"Town-level beads: Real DB for coordination mail","description":"Implement Option A from mail redesign: Town gets real beads DB for coordination.

## Background

Mail is now Beads. But currently:
- Town .beads/redirect points to rig beads
- mayor/mail/ has legacy JSONL files
- Cross-rig coordination has no clear home

## Design

Town beads = coordination, cross-rig mail, mayor inbox, handoffs
Rig beads = project issues, work items

Matches HOP hierarchy: platform > project > worker

## Structure

~/gt/
  .beads/           # REAL beads DB (prefix: gm-)
  mayor/
    town.json
    state.json      # NO mail/ directory
  gastown/
    .beads/         # Rig beads (prefix: ga-)

## Tasks

1. Delete ~/gt/.beads/redirect
2. Run bd init --prefix gm at ~/gt/ (town beads)
3. Delete ~/gt/mayor/mail/ directory
4. Update gt mail to use beads not JSONL
5. Add mail fields (thread_id, reply_to, msg_type)
6. Update gt prime for two-tier model
7. Update docs/architecture.md

## Addressing

- mayor/ -> town beads
- rig/agent -> rig beads
- Cross-rig -> town beads","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T19:09:55.855955-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-jzot","title":"gt done: Notify Witness with exit outcome","description":"When polecat runs gt done, it should send mail to Witness:

```
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'POLECAT_DONE <name>' -m 'Exit: COMPLETED
Issue: <issue-id>
MR: <mr-id>
Branch: <branch>'
```

Exit types:
- COMPLETED: Work done, MR submitted
- ESCALATED: Hit blocker, needs human
- DEFERRED: Work paused, issue still open

This enables Witness patrol to:
1. See completion in inbox-check step
2. Verify git state is clean
3. Kill session and prune worktree
4. Close the polecat lease in its patrol wisp

Paired with gt-r6td (spawn notification) - together they bracket polecat lifecycle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:31.266716-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:55:15.215207-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:55:15.215207-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}

 {"id":"gt-lek6","title":"gt rig reset --stale: Clear orphaned in_progress items","description":"Reset in_progress issues when their assigned agent no longer exists.

## Problem
When polecats die without cleanup, their issues remain in_progress forever.
Need a way to bulk-reset these orphaned items.

## Command
```bash
gt rig reset --stale [--dry-run]
```

## Logic
For each in_progress issue in rig:
1. Parse assignee (e.g., "gastown/furiosa")
2. Map to tmux session name (gt-gastown-furiosa)
3. If session does NOT exist:
   - Reset status to "open"
   - Clear assignee
4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)
   OR check if crew tmux session exists

## Output
```
Resetting stale work in gastown:
  gt-abc: gastown/furiosa (no session) → open
  gt-def: gastown/nux (no session) → open
  Skipped: gt-xyz: gastown/crew/max (persistent)
Reset 2 issues, skipped 1
```

## Related
- gt-2kz: CLI cleanup commands for stale state
- gt-rdmw: orphan-check in deacon patrol
- gt-orphans command (list orphaned molecules)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:46.962413-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --stale flag for gt rig reset with dry-run support"}

 {"id":"gt-lnji","title":"gt polecat git-state command for pre-kill verification","description":"Add git-state subcommand to gt polecat for Witness pre-kill verification.

```bash
gt polecat git-state <rig>/<polecat>
```

```
Git State: gastown/furiosa

  Working Tree: clean | dirty
  Uncommitted:  0 files | N files (list)
  Unpushed:     0 commits | N commits ahead
  Stashes:      0 | N stashes

  Verdict: CLEAN (safe to kill) | DIRTY (needs cleanup)
```

## JSON output (--json flag)
```json
{
  "clean": true,
  "uncommitted_files": [],
  "unpushed_commits": 0,
  "stash_count": 0
}
```

## Used by
- Witness pre-kill verification (mol-witness-patrol)
- Manual cleanup checks

## Implementation
- Check git status in polecat worktree
- Check git log origin/main..HEAD
- Check git stash list","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:10.035052-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:35:06.34384-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:35:06.34384-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt polecat git-state command for pre-kill verification"}
 {"id":"gt-lno","title":"Swarm state persistence: manifest + state + events","description":"Upgrade swarm persistence to match PGT pattern.

## Current State
Single .gastown/swarms.json with all swarms.

## Target State (per-swarm)
```
.gastown/swarms/
└── <swarm-id>/
    ├── manifest.json  # Immutable config
    ├── state.json     # Mutable progress
    ├── events.jsonl   # Audit log
    └── report.md      # Generated report
```

## File Schemas

### manifest.json (immutable after creation)
```json
{
  "id": "sw-1",
  "title": "Epic description",
  "epic_id": "gt-abc",
  "rig": "gastown",
  "base_commit": "abc123",
  "integration_branch": "swarm/sw-1",
  "target_branch": "main",
  "workers": ["Toast", "Nux"],
  "tasks": [{"id": "gt-xyz", "title": "..."}],
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### state.json (mutable)
```json
{
  "state": "active",
  "task_states": {
    "gt-xyz": {"state": "merged", "assignee": "Toast"}
  },
  "updated_at": "2024-01-01T01:00:00Z",
  "error": null
}
```

### events.jsonl (append-only audit)
```jsonl
{"event": "created", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_assigned", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_merged", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
```

## Benefits
- Audit trail via events.jsonl
- Manifest immutability prevents corruption
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Per-swarm isolation

## Migration
Keep backward compat with swarms.json during transition.

## Files to Modify
- internal/swarm/manager.go: Refactor persistence
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore → directory-based

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-swarm directory structure
- [ ] Events logged to JSONL
- [ ] Manifest immutable after creation
- [ ] List command aggregates from directories","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:14.151538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00"}
